mnemonics: Gradients in Deep Networks: Gradient = derivative of output w.r.t. input
Quadratic Cost: Quadratic = (weight - bias)^2, Gradients are the derivative of this
Descending the Gradient: Decrease weight/bias by the negative gradient, Go Down the Gradient
Gradi: Gradient Descent Accelerated, Gradient Descent is Accelerated
keyPoints: Backpropagation is a method for computing gradients in deep networks
Automatic differentiation is used to compute gradients in backpropagation
The gradient of a quadratic cost function is the derivative of the cost function
The gradient descent algorithm is used to descend the gradient of the cost function
Gradient descent is a widely used optimization technique in deep learning
